CIPHER.550.cipherString: Before a stranger do nothing that should be kept secret, for you do not know what it will produce later on.
CIPHER.550.question: While scrolling through an old floppy disk labeled "TOP SECRET", you discovered this quote hidden as an Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.547.cipherString: In mathematics, in science, and in life, we constantly face the delicate, tricky task of separating design from happenstance.
CIPHER.547.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Ivars Peterson, encoded as a Patristocrat using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with INM. What does it say?
CIPHER.547.author: Ivars Peterson

CIPHER.534.cipherString: The biggest problem is people are afraid of poetry, think they can't understand it or that it will be boring.
CIPHER.534.question: Hidden in the margin of a library book is this Aristocrat encoded quote by Caroline Kennedy. You are told that the cipher contains the word IT twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.534.author: Caroline Kennedy

CIPHER.537.cipherString: If listening was as important as speaking, you would have twice as many ears as mouths.
CIPHER.537.question: Ron English offers us some advice that has been encoded as a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of SPECULATION. What does it say?
CIPHER.537.author: Ron English

CIPHER.524.cipherString: How true it is when they say there is nothing which makes a man more furious than the discovery that he has deceived himself!
CIPHER.524.question: Your Bunsen burner's flame suddenly spelled this Patristocrat message by Michael Moorcock using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains HOW. What does it say?
CIPHER.524.author: Michael Moorcock

CIPHER.541.question: Decrypt the following cipher text that has been encoded using the Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 218465 3826 7096 decode to?

CIPHER.528.cipherString: Besides the fact that I make movies, there's nothing interesting about my life at all, unfortunately.
CIPHER.528.question: A phrase by Jonah Hill has been encoded as an Aristocrat with a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.528.author: Jonah Hill

CIPHER.523.cipherString: Organizationally what is required - and evolving - is systems management.
CIPHER.523.question: Projected by a broken laser pointer during physics class, solve this Nihilist Substitution quote from Peter Drucker with a keyword length of 4. The 31st through 41st cipher units (75 56 66 65 96 47 57 83 65 56 56) decode to be ANDEVOLVING. What does it say?
CIPHER.523.author: Peter Drucker

CIPHER.549.cipherString: By attending to the conscious part of ourselves, we contribute to the peace of others as well as ourselves.
CIPHER.549.question: Your lab partner's retainer case clicked open to reveal this Aristocrat-encrypted quote by Warren Farrell. What does it say?
CIPHER.549.author: Warren Farrell

CIPHER.531.cipherString: The beginning of thought is in disagreement -- not only with others but also with ourselves.
CIPHER.531.question: You receive an anonymous text message containing a coded passage. It's said to be a quote by Eric Hoffer, encrypted with a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of OBSERVING. What does it say?
CIPHER.531.author: Eric Hoffer

CIPHER.532.cipherString: Siempre son los aventureros los que hacen grandes cosas, no los soberanos de los grandes imperios.
CIPHER.532.question: You intercept a mysterious radio signal. Decoding it reveals a quote by Montesquieu in Spanish encrypted as a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.532.author: Montesquieu
CIPHER.532.translation: It is always adventurers who do great things, not the sovereigns of great empires.

CIPHER.543.cipherString: I still can't believe it, but it looks like it was a big deal.
CIPHER.543.question: A laser-etched message on the inside of a geodes crystal reads: solve this Nihilist Substitution version of Rayssa Leal's wisdom with a keyword of ABOUND and Polybius key of REDUCTION. What does it say?
CIPHER.543.author: Rayssa Leal

CIPHER.522.cipherString: La vida del hombre nunca fue un deporte para él; Era una dura realidad, un asunto muy serio para estar vivo.
CIPHER.522.question: Found inside a hollowed-out periodic table book: this Xenocrypt Cipher in Spanish hides Thomas Carlyle's insight using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.522.author: Thomas Carlyle
CIPHER.522.translation: Man's life never was a sport to him; it was a stern reality--altogether a serious matter to be alive.

CIPHER.542.cipherString: I would like to be judged on the validity of my arguments, not as a victim.
CIPHER.542.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Ayaan Hirsi Ali encoded as a Checkerboard. The deciphered text starts with IWOULDL. What does it say?
CIPHER.542.author: Ayaan Hirsi Ali

CIPHER.544.cipherString: There are only two lasting bequests we can hope to give our children. One of these is roots, the other, wings.
CIPHER.544.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Hodding Carter encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.544.author: Hodding Carter

CIPHER.533.cipherString: For this reason poetry is something more philosophical and more worthy of serious attention than history.
CIPHER.533.question: A fragment of text is discovered in a time capsule from 1965. It's a quote by Aristotle encrypted as a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has ETRYI somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.533.author: Aristotle

CIPHER.530.cipherString: The mightiest works have been accomplished by men who have kept their ability to dream great dreams.
CIPHER.530.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Walter Bowie using the Aristocrat Cipher with a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.530.author: Walter Bowie

CIPHER.529.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Cryptarithm code. What do the values 30459 2872 1866 decode to?

CIPHER.526.cipherString: If our opinions rest upon solid ground, those who attack them do not make us angry, but themselves ridiculous.
CIPHER.526.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Aristocrat-encrypted quote by John Lancaster Spalding. What does it say?
CIPHER.526.author: John Lancaster Spalding

CIPHER.548.cipherString: Myth therefore seems to choose history, rather than be chosen by it.
CIPHER.548.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Porta message onto the screen—decode François-Bernard Mâche words using a keyword of ARGUED. What does it say?
CIPHER.548.author: François-Bernard Mâche

CIPHER.539.cipherString: Each separate star seems nothing, but a myriad scattered stars break up the night and make it beautiful.
CIPHER.539.question: While repairing an old telescope, you find a note hidden in the tube. The message is a quote written in an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?

CIPHER.535.cipherString: Human-machine symbiosis is the workforce of the future.
CIPHER.535.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by Newton Lee encoded as a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.535.author: Newton Lee

CIPHER.536.cipherString: Of course not, she agreed, You are nothing if not exhaustive in your self-congratulatory made-up logic.
CIPHER.536.question: Someone left a paper with the message below on the copy machine in the office. It's a quote by Brandon Sanderson, encoded as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word NOT twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.536.author: Brandon Sanderson

CIPHER.546.cipherString: I have a new philosophy. I'm only going to dread one day at a time.
CIPHER.546.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: this Baconian Cipher by Charles M. Schulz. What does it say?
CIPHER.546.author: Charles M. Schulz

CIPHER.521.cipherString: Friends, I have lost a day.
CIPHER.521.question: A quote by Emperor Titus has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of BUNT for you to decode. What does it say?
CIPHER.521.author: Emperor Titus

CIPHER.538.cipherString: Every case stands upon its own bottom.
CIPHER.538.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Baconian banner. You are told that the deciphered text starts with EVER. What does it say?

CIPHER.545.cipherString: I'm always making a comeback but nobody ever tells me where I've been.
CIPHER.545.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Billie Holiday, written using a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword of GREATS and Polybius key of LONGEVITY. What does it say?
CIPHER.545.author: Billie Holiday

CIPHER.525.cipherString: Truth from the mouth of an honest man and severity from a good-natured man have a double effect.
CIPHER.525.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Complete Columnar cipher text by Hazlitt. The quote has MANAN somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.525.author: Hazlitt

CIPHER.540.cipherString: Life's like art. You have to work hard to keep it simple and still have meaning.
CIPHER.540.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Porta encoded quote by Charles de Lint. The 49th through 55th cipher characters (QYRUSGZ) decode to be ILLHAVE. What does it say?
CIPHER.540.author: Charles de Lint

CIPHER.527.cipherString: There's always a source for humor.
CIPHER.527.question: Your school's intercom crackled to life with static… then spelled out this Hill message by Calvin Trillin with a keyword of SUBFIELDS. What does it say?
CIPHER.527.author: Calvin Trillin
